etymology.description: 揚(揚) (yáng, "to raise; to spread; to fly") + 州(州) (zhōu, "prefecture; state") = Yangzhou, a city historically known for its elevation and significance as a regional hub.
etymology.interpretation: 揚(揚) (yáng, "to raise; to spread; to fly") suggests a rising or spreading influence, often linked to its status as a cultural and economic center in ancient times. 州(州) (zhōu, "prefecture; state") refers to an administrative division. The combination of these morphemes describes a place of elevated status and regional importance, reflecting its historical role as a flourishing trade city.

parts.english: to raise; to spread; to fly

examples.chinese: 扬州(揚州)是一个美丽的城市(是一個美麗的城市)。
examples.pinyin: Yángzhōu shì yīgè měilì de chéngshì.

examples.chinese: 春天到扬州时(春天到揚州時)，你会看到许多花开(你會看到許多花開)。
examples.pinyin: Chūntiān dào Yángzhōu shí, nǐ huì kàn dào xǔduō huā kāi.
examples.english: In spring when you arrive in Yangzhou, you will see many flowers blooming.

examples.chinese: 我们计划去扬州旅游(我們計劃去揚州旅遊)。
examples.english: We plan to travel to Yangzhou.
